description_long: This object is a blocky, L-shaped structure composed of dark, weathered stone bricks, reminiscent of old masonry or fortress walls. The surface is uneven, with visible mortar lines and a rough, aged texture, suggesting exposure to the elements. The bricks are arranged in a staggered pattern, with some stones appearing slightly chipped or worn, adding to the sense of realism. The top surfaces are flat and broad, while the vertical section rises abruptly, forming a right angle. There are no visible openings, labels, or decorative elements, and the entire object is uniformly constructed from the same stone material. The overall appearance is robust and utilitarian, suitable for use as a barrier, bench, or architectural feature in an urban environment.
description: A robust, L-shaped stone structure made of dark, weathered bricks with a rough, aged surface and no visible openings or labels.
description_view_0: A narrow end view showing the depth of the stone bricks and the vertical rise of the L-shape.
description_view_1: A side view revealing the full L-shaped profile and the staggered brick pattern along the length.
description_view_2: A compact end view displaying the width and stacked arrangement of the stone bricks.
description_view_3: The opposite side view, again showing the L-shaped profile and the rough, blocky stone texture.
